repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 reports no completed PartialID entry; pid_manski1990test is unrelated to two-study data-fusion compatibility.

published_prior_art.cite: LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025 §4 already states pointwise interval compatibility conditions and tests their expectation proxy; it does not state a support-clipped common-latent-extension iff.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; the repository has no completed learned-nuisance PartialID compatibility-set inference result.

published_prior_art.cite: MastenPoirier2020 gives directional-delta numerical-bootstrap uniform frontier inference, while ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013, FangSantos2019, and HongLi2018 provide component methods but not this learned-nuisance, tie-inclusive two-study compatibility-zero-set result.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / comparator_promise_table for LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited paper's relevant population results are Lemma 4 and Theorem 4 in §4, not “Lemma 1” and “Theorem 1”; repair the claimed location anchors while retaining the otherwise accurate pointwise-inequality comparison.

novelty_flags.label: oeq:exact-compatibility / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: Add YuKarmakarVandeleestSchwarz2024, “Using a Two-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Framework to Efficiently Combine Randomized and Non-randomized Studies” (arXiv:2412.03731; now listed as JRSS B in press), a materially closer omitted two-parameter trial/observational fusion comparator.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct Lanners et al.'s result-number anchors and add Yu et al. (2024/2026), explicitly distinguishing its maintained assumptions and target from the proposed common-extension compatibility region.

Promote the cancellation calculation into a theorem-level strict comparison over a stated nondegenerate class: both published signed tests accept while C_P>0, then show how K(P) changes the resulting causal bound.

Specify a Hölder-driven local separation rate and local-power alternative for the contact-set procedure, with an explicit theorem-level contrast to Masten--Poirier's frontier bands and Lanners et al.'s fixed-nuisance resampling.

recommended_next_step: Repair the Lanners anchors and add the omitted Yu et al. comparator; the exact-extension plus noncancellation kernel is otherwise plausibly field-tier and ready for Stage 0.
